```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Unemployment Insurance Claims Spike Bearish Rotation",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims) weekly via fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly jobless claims spike above 4-week moving average signals labor market deterioration, triggering defensive rotation out of cyclicals.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary is most sensitive to employment stress; claims spikes precede consumer pullback."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When ICSA exceeds its 4-week MA by >8% and closes above prior week",
      "exit": "After 3 weeks or when ICSA falls below 4-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Claims data is volatile and seasonal; threshold breaches occur 2-4 times per quarter on average."
  },
  {

    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",

  {
```
